channel.title: Careers on Franklin Peñaranda | coffemanfp
channel.description: Recent content in Careers on Franklin Peñaranda | coffemanfp

item.title: YoFio
item.description: YoFio is a financial start-up that provides users with credit lines based on their credit history to help them with their financial issues. Since I started the idea of my entry was to migrate the logic of a credit to a line of credit. With no financial knowledge, I had to learn everything about how a finance company, a line of credit and the business logic itself worked in order to create the most appropriate use cases.

item.title: Chat
item.description: Chat is literally another fucking chat. It is a personal project that I created to create my own challenges, define my knowledge and learn new technologies. The goal is that it works as an individual platform and that it can also be part of other projects created by myself that will form a mini social network. This is the beginning of a great project that will define my professional career.

item.title: Fyld Inc
item.description: Fyld Inc. is a start-up that aims to provide its users with a mail witnessing service. My entry as a Full-Stack developer started with a turnaround in the whole code architecture. I developed the migration from a monolithic architecture to a microservices architecture. Being a mail witnessing service, the priority is to back up the information sent in the mails of the sending users. It works in such a way that a sender user sends an email to the original recipients and additionally adds Fyld among its recipients.

item.title: Tenla City
item.description: Tenla City is an E-Commerce platform that facilitates the purchase and sale of products to customers and sellers. With its developed management of promotions, coupons and discounts, it keeps users interested in the most popular stores in the city. Users also have the facility to create a meeting point to meet other users in the store they are in. Tenla City is a project for the city of Maracaibo, Zulia, Venezuela.
